# Lila Park — Circadian Wellness
> Lila Park (이라 박) is a Korean-American circadian wellness researcher and writer. She applies Korean saenghwal (생활) traditions — everyday living practices — to modern sleep science, nutrition, and nervous system health. Based in Seoul → San Francisco.

## About Lila
Lila holds a background in biomedical research and spent years in Seoul studying traditional Korean wellness practices before moving to SF. Her philosophy: feeling great doesn't require suffering. Small, consistent changes compound. Sleep is the foundation — fix that and everything else gets easier.

## Core Site
- [Home](https://lilapark.xyz/): Lila's story, featured articles, free sleep cheat sheet
- [Journal](https://lilapark.xyz/journal): All articles — sleep science, Korean food, energy, nervous system, Korean wellness
- [Products](https://lilapark.xyz/products): The 3am Protocol — 28-day deep sleep programme ($17)
- [About](https://lilapark.xyz/about): Lila's background and methodology

## Free Resource
- [Sleep Cheat Sheet](https://lilapark.xyz/): Free download — 7 sleep optimisation principles from circadian science and Korean saenghwal. Subscribe via The 3am Letters newsletter.

## Product
- [The 3am Protocol](https://lilapark.xyz/products): $17 — 28-day programme for deep sleep using circadian biology and Korean saenghwal practices. Covers light exposure, meal timing, evening wind-down, Korean herbal practices, and stress physiology.

## Articles
- [Why You Can't Catch Up on Sleep (And What Actually Works)](https://lilapark.xyz/article): Sleep debt science — the recovery window most people miss
- [Why You're Exhausted Even After 8 Hours](https://lilapark.xyz/exhausted-after-8-hours): Sleep architecture and quality vs duration
- [I Made Kimchi Fried Rice Healthy Without Ruining It](https://lilapark.xyz/kimchi-fried-rice-healthy): Korean food nutrition — what to change and what to leave alone
- [The 5-Minute Morning Habit That Changed My Whole Day](https://lilapark.xyz/morning-habit-5-minutes): Circadian morning light protocol
- [You Don't Need the Gym — You Need to Move Like a Korean Grandma](https://lilapark.xyz/move-like-korean-grandma): Korean movement culture — everyday activity vs structured exercise
- [The 3pm Crash Isn't Normal — It's a Timing Problem](https://lilapark.xyz/3pm-crash-not-normal): Circadian rhythm and afternoon energy dip
- [Your Gut Is Your Second Brain — But Not in the Way Influencers Mean](https://lilapark.xyz/gut-second-brain): Gut-brain axis — the evidence vs the hype
- [Nunchi: The Korean Art of Reading the Room](https://lilapark.xyz/nunchi-korean-art): 눈치 (nunchi) as a nervous system and social attunement practice
- [Perimenopause Ruined My Sleep. Here's What Actually Helped.](https://lilapark.xyz/perimenopause-sleep): Hormonal sleep disruption — what works beyond the mainstream and the supplement industry
- [4-7-8 Breathing Is Fine. This Is Better.](https://lilapark.xyz/breathwork-better-than-478): More precise breathwork protocol for nervous system downregulation

## Newsletter
- The 3am Letters: Weekly sleep and circadian wellness insight. Every Sunday. 2,400+ readers. Subscribe at lilapark.xyz.

## Key Concepts Lila Covers
- Circadian biology and chronobiology
- Korean saenghwal (생활) — everyday wellness practices
- Yangsaeng (양생) — the Korean art of nourishing life
- Sleep debt and sleep architecture
- Gut-brain axis and the enteric nervous system
- Nunchi (눈치) — Korean social and nervous system attunement
- Jujube tea, acupressure, and Korean herbal traditions
- Perimenopause and hormonal effects on sleep
- Breathwork and nervous system regulation
